conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: My laptop fan has been louder than usual, might need cleaning.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: The dentist appointment I've been putting off is finally scheduled.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: HavenAdapter.search() currently returns MemoryEngine.query()'s single formatted context string, with type/confidence/importance/valid_from annotations baked in, as the sole result entry.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: I need to return that package before the return window closes.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: We could keep returning that annotated string -- it's already implemented and the judge scores it fine.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: The wifi in the coffee shop I like to work from has been spotty.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: That hands the benchmark's judge a structurally richer answer than every other adapter gets -- mem0 and the baselines all return raw, unannotated text. Switch to one raw canonical_fact entry per accepted candidate instead.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: I finally got around to donating some old clothes I never wear.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: My neighbor's dog barked most of the afternoon, hard to get anything done with the window open.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Constraint: the RetrievalTrace object itself must never be passed to, or serialized into, an LLM prompt -- only its candidates' canonical_fact strings can leave this adapter.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: The bus was running about ten minutes late again this morning.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Blocked: query_with_trace's candidate order needs to exactly reproduce what ContextBuilder would have rendered, or the switch silently changes which facts the judge sees.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: I finally fixed the wobbly chair leg in the kitchen.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: There's a new bakery opening down the street next month.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Confirmed RetrievalTrace.candidates is already produced in DeterministicRanker's final-score order, the same order DeterministicSlotAllocator re-derives -- filtering to accepted entries reproduces the right set and order. Unblocks the switch.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: My phone battery has been draining faster than usual lately.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Rewrote HavenAdapter.search() to call query_with_trace and map accepted candidates to {id, memory: canonical_fact}.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: I need to renew my passport before it expires this winter.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Updated the adapter's tests to assert no confidence:/valid_from: annotations ever appear in a result's memory field.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: The gym was way more crowded than usual today.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: I switched to a different brand of coffee and I'm not sure I like it.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Open question: does returning one entry per candidate instead of one joined block distort retrieved_memories-count-based diagnostics elsewhere?

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: It's supposed to rain most of next week according to the forecast.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Next: check classify_failure.py's NO_RETRIEVAL heuristic against the new per-candidate result count.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: I still haven't unpacked the last box from the move.

ground_truth.current_objective: Fix HavenAdapter.search() so it stops leaking metadata annotations into the benchmark judge's answer.
active_decisions: Return one raw canonical_fact entry per accepted candidate from query_with_trace, not a single annotated formatted-context string.
RetrievalTrace.candidates' existing final-score order (from DeterministicRanker) is reused as-is when filtering to accepted entries.
superseded_or_rejected: Keeping the annotated formatted-context string as the adapter's search() result.
constraints: The RetrievalTrace object itself must never be passed to, or serialized into, an LLM prompt.
active_tasks: Check classify_failure.py's NO_RETRIEVAL heuristic against the new per-candidate result count.
open_questions: Does returning one entry per candidate distort other retrieved_memories-count-based diagnostics?
ground_truth.top_priority_next_action: Check whether classify_failure.py's NO_RETRIEVAL heuristic still behaves correctly now that search() returns one entry per accepted candidate.
resolved_blockers: Whether query_with_trace's candidate order reproduces what ContextBuilder would have rendered.

queries.text: What should we work on next?

must_state: Return one raw canonical_fact entry per accepted candidate from query_with_trace, not a single annotated formatted-context string.
RetrievalTrace.candidates' existing final-score order (from DeterministicRanker) is reused as-is when filtering to accepted entries.
The RetrievalTrace object itself must never be passed to, or serialized into, an LLM prompt.
must_not_state: Keeping the annotated formatted-context string as the adapter's search() result, as a current or recommended approach
Whether query_with_trace's candidate order reproduces what ContextBuilder would have rendered, as still open/blocking
forbidden_actions: Recommending that RetrievalTrace be serialized directly into an LLM prompt for debugging.
must_prioritize: Check whether classify_failure.py's NO_RETRIEVAL heuristic still behaves correctly now that search() returns one entry per accepted candidate.
